root_motivation: Composition of multiple bi-asset.v1 bundles into a dashboard layout is a deterministic fold over existing data — not a generative step — so it should be a standalone pure-Rust tool that LLMs and embedded apps can invoke without network or render dependencies.
user_persona: An LLM orchestrator or embedded BI app that holds N bi-asset.v1 bundles from the mqo bi-toolkit chain and needs to assemble them into a renderable dashboard spec or a grid-positioned manifest for a self-rendering client.

acceptance_criteria.description: Two bi-asset.v1 bundles compose into a dashboard.v1 with two panels, each panel's title preserved from its source bundle.
Grid layout with columns=2 places panel 0 at (row 0, col 0), panel 1 at (row 0, col 1), panel 2 at (row 1, col 0).
Vertical layout emits a vega_concat_spec whose vconcat array holds the panel vega_specs in input order.
Each panel caption incorporates the source bundle's description and any caveats.
Zero panels → a structured error with a nonzero exit code and no panic.
One panel → a valid single-panel dashboard.v1 with one entry in panels and a one-element concat array.
Panel ordering and grid placement are deterministic across repeated runs on identical input.

scope: Read N bi-asset.v1 JSON bundles from --assets (array file) and/or --asset (individual files)
Compute row/col grid positions for each panel (grid/vertical/horizontal layouts)
Compose panel captions from bundle description + caveats
Emit dashboard.v1 JSON manifest with panels array and vega_concat_spec
Nonzero exit on zero panels (structured error, no panic)
non_goals: Rendering the concat spec to PNG/SVG

five_whys_trace.q: Why does this CLI need to exist when an LLM could hand-assemble a concat spec?
five_whys_trace.a: Because the bundles already carry title/description/vega_spec/caveats — a generative step would re-derive what's already encoded, adding noise.

five_whys_trace.q: Why emit both dashboard.v1 and vega_concat_spec rather than one form?
five_whys_trace.a: Two kinds of client: a Vega-Lite engine wants one renderable spec; a grid-owning client wants panel positions as data.

five_whys_trace.q: Why make this a separate crate rather than a function in mqo-bi-asset-bundle?
five_whys_trace.a: Composition is a different concern from bundling a single asset; keeping it separate keeps both crates focused and independently testable.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Caption format when caveats present: separator?
bi-asset.v1 schema definition — use inline vs crate dep?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Inline — mqo-bi-asset-bundle may not be published yet; PRD explicitly allows inline definition.
